feat: add gitvault get/set commands for single key-value access (REQ-116)

Implements `gitvault get <FILE> <KEY>` and `gitvault set <FILE> <KEY>
[VALUE|--stdin]` for reading and updating individual key values in
sealed and encrypted files.

Sealed-file mode (JSON/YAML/TOML/.env):
- `get`: unseal file → extract dot-path key → print plaintext
- `set`: unseal → update key (upsert) → re-seal atomically

Store-file mode (.age):
- `get`: decrypt archive → extract key from detected format → print
- `set`: decrypt → update key → re-encrypt atomically

Key features:
- Dot-path navigation for JSON/YAML/TOML (e.g. `db.password`)
- Variable name for .env files
- `--stdin` flag for secrets (avoids shell history exposure)
- `--json` output: `{"file":..,"key":..,"value":..}`
- TOML updates via `toml_edit` to preserve comments/formatting
- .env updates preserve all comments, blank lines, and variable order
- Upsert semantics: creates top-level key if missing; nested path
  creation rejected with a helpful error
- Store-file format auto-detected from .age file stem extension
- All plaintext bytes zeroized before return
- 22 unit tests + 1 integration test (full seal→get→set→get round-trip)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

## Summary

Add comprehensive contract test suites for three analysis enricher
crates, covering **116 tests** total.

### Test files

| Crate | File | Tests |

**Derived** — totals aggregation, doc density, whitespace ratio,
verbosity rate, distribution statistics (min/max/mean/median/gini),

polyglot metrics (entropy, dominant lang), histogram buckets, integrity

(proptest).

**Complexity** — histogram generation (boundary values, bucket spread),
\ uild_complexity_report\ via temp-dir fixtures (Rust, JavaScript,
Python, Go), function counting, cyclomatic ordering, detail functions
flag, edge cases (empty/binary/single-line files), missing file graceful
handling, byte limits, determinism, JSON serialization, property-based
invariants.

**Near-dup** — identical/different/partial-overlap detection, scope
modes (Global/Lang/Module), threshold behavior, clustering
(representative, sorted files, pair count), truncation (\max_pairs\),
glob exclusion patterns, params metadata, runtime stats, determinism,
sorted pairs, JSON serialization, property-based invariants (similarity
∈ [0,1], shared fingerprints ≤ min individual).

### Patterns followed
- BTreeMap (not HashMap)
- \(a..=b).contains(&x)\ ranges
- proptest for invariant verification
- BDD-style Given/When/Then naming
- tempfile fixtures for I/O tests

commit.message:
fix: use system allocator for Swift-allocated AudioBufferList deallocation (#132)

AudioBufferList::drop() used Vec::from_raw_parts to free the buffers
array allocated by Swift via UnsafeMutablePointer.allocate(). This
routes through Rust's global allocator, which crashes when consumers
use a custom allocator (mimalloc, jemalloc, etc.) since the memory
was allocated by Swift's system malloc.

Replace with std::alloc::System.dealloc() to always match Swift's
allocation path regardless of the consumer's #[global_allocator].

Add a detecting-allocator integration test that proves:
- Vec::from_raw_parts routes through the global allocator (the bug)
- System.dealloc bypasses the global allocator (the fix)

Closes #129

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Implemented dynamic port allocation: the server now auto-retries on
EADDRINUSE (up to 10 ports) and prints `FLIGHTDECK_PORT=NNNN` to stdout
for easier discovery and support for multiple concurrent instances.

script with a new sequential launcher (`scripts/dev.mjs`) that starts
Express first, captures the actual port, and then starts Vite with the
correct proxy target. This allows multiple dev instances to run
simultaneously.

* Enforced commit sign-off conventions: all agent commits now include
agent ID, role, and model name in a `Signed-off-by` trailer, and
documentation reflects this new behavior.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-925cb0f14a391cdbbe596568bcce00a8ba0d5ec3517c0de3733d8bbe0c8c6f4bL242-R245)

* Removed redundant tool call activity cards from the agent chat panel
to streamline the UI.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-a369183eee2a0811facf550756fcb97a1c8a9271d07c705ef4f4bce4bfa82ce7L4-R4)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-a369183eee2a0811facf550756fcb97a1c8a9271d07c705ef4f4bce4bfa82ce7L26-L114)

* Updated documentation and changelog to reflect these changes and
removed the `.flightdeck/port` file mechanism in favor of stdout-based
port discovery.

These changes collectively enhance both the user and developer
experience, making the system more robust, visually intuitive, and
easier to work with.

feat(wasm): add tokmd-io-port crate with ReadFs trait and MemFs (#510)

## Summary

Introduces okmd-io-port, a new microcrate with trait-based I/O ports
that abstract filesystem access. This is a prerequisite for WASM support
— it allows okmd-scan and other crates to operate against in-memory data
instead of requiring a real filesystem.

## What's included

| Type | Name | Purpose |
|------|------|---------|
| Trait | \ReadFs\ | Read-only filesystem port (\ ead_to_string\, \
ead_bytes\, \xists\, \is_dir\, \is_file\) |
| Struct | \HostFs\ | Default implementation delegating to \std::fs\ |
| Struct | \MemFs\ | \BTreeMap\-backed in-memory filesystem for tests
and WASM |

## Test coverage

14 unit tests covering both implementations (HostFs and MemFs),
including error paths (not found, invalid UTF-8).

## What's NOT included

This is a **new crate with no consumers yet**. Wiring \ReadFs\ into \
okmd-scan\ and \ okmd-walk\ will follow in a separate PR.

## Checklist

- [x] New crate added to workspace \members\ and
\workspace.dependencies\
- [x] \cargo fmt\ clean
- [x] \cargo test -p tokmd-io-port\ — 14/14 passing
- [x] No existing crates modified (additive only)

commit.message:
fix: compute lrecl_fixed for ODO schemas using max_count allocation (#333)

ODO schemas in fixed-format COBOL files are padded to max LRECL.
The layout resolver already computed current_offset using max_count,
but calculate_fixed_record_length() skipped storing it when ODO was
present. This fix removes that guard, always storing the computed LRECL.

- Remove 10 #[ignore] from e2e tests now that ODO+fixed works
- Fix encode_with_dialect_flag test (--output flag was missing)

* feat(merge): add automated merge conflict resolution system

Add a multi-layered merge conflict resolution system:

1. Custom git merge drivers in .gitattributes — auto-resolves ~80% of
   conflicts on generated/framework-managed files by accepting upstream.

2. Sync engine integration — syncGitattributes() appends managed merge
   driver rules to .gitattributes (preserving user EOL config) on every
   agentkit sync, so downstream repos inherit merge drivers automatically.

3. Executable resolution scripts (sh + ps1) — scripts/resolve-merge.sh
   codifies the merge resolution matrix: auto-resolves generated files,
   lockfiles, and configs; flags engine/spec files for manual review.

4. GitHub Actions workflow — merge-conflict-detection.yml triggers on
   pushes to dev/main, checks all open PRs for conflicts, and posts
   categorised PR comments with resolution instructions.

5. CLAUDE.md template policy — teaches Claude Code the merge resolution
   matrix so it resolves conflicts automatically without manual prompting.

commit.message:
fix(ci): exclude copybook-bdd from nextest in perf and pr-insights workflows (#337)

Cucumber BDD exits with code 104 when scenarios are skipped, which
causes nextest to report failure. CI Quick and ci.yml already exclude
copybook-bdd from nextest (running BDD separately via
governance-bdd-smoke.sh). Apply the same exclusion to perf.yml and
pr-insights.yml.

Also excludes copybook-bench from nextest in these workflows (it's a
bench-only crate, not a test crate).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
